content.problemStatement:
Chef has KKK chocolates and he wants to distribute them to NNN people (numbered 111 through NNN). These people are standing in a line in such a way that for each iii (1≤i≤N−11 \le i \le N-11≤i≤N−1), person iii and person i+1i+1i+1 are adjacent.

First, consider some way to distribute chocolates such that for each valid iii, the number of chocolates the iii-th person would receive from Chef is AiA_iAi​ and the sum S1=∑i=1N−1∣Ai−Ai+1∣S_1 = \sum_{i=1}^{N-1} \left|A_i - A_{i+1}\right|S1​=∑i=1N−1​∣Ai​−Ai+1​∣ is minimum possible. Of course, each person must receive a non-negative integer number of chocolates.

Then, Chef wants to create a new sequence B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ by rearranging (permuting) the elements of the sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. For each valid iii, the number of chocolates the iii-th person actually receives from Chef is BiB_iBi​. Chef wants to distribute the chocolates (choose B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ by permuting the sequence AAA and give BiB_iBi​ chocolates to the iii-th person for each valid iii) in such a way that S2=∑i=1N−1∣Bi–Bi+1∣S_2 = \sum_{i=1}^{N-1} \left|B_i – B_{i+1}\right|S2​=∑i=1N−1​∣Bi​–Bi+1​∣ is **maximum** possible. You need to find the maximum value of S2S_2S2​.

It is guaranteed that S2S_2S2​ does not depend on the exact choice of the sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, as long as it is a sequence that minimises S1S_1S1​.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a single integer KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum value of the sum S2S_2S2​.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: To minimise S1S_1S1​, Chef could give 111 chocolate to person 111 and 111 chocolate to person 222, so S1=∣1−1∣+∣1−0∣=1S_1 = |1-1| + |1-0| = 1S1​=∣1−1∣+∣1−0∣=1.
To maximise S2S_2S2​, Chef can give 111 chocolate to person 111 and 111 chocolate to person 333, since the sequence B=(1,0,1)B = (1, 0, 1)B=(1,0,1) is a permutation of A=(1,1,0)A = (1, 1, 0)A=(1,1,0). Then, S2=∣1−0∣+∣0−1∣=2S_2 = |1-0| + |0-1| = 2S2​=∣1−0∣+∣0−1∣=2.
